Ad Text:
12 months Contract | Canberra, ACT
Security Clearance: Baseline (minimum required)

Overview

Alchemy Partners is an Australian-owned and led boutique consulting firm that partners with clients to improve business performance through better collaboration. We help organisations navigate complex operating environments by bringing structure, clarity and practical insight to their most challenging problems.

We are currently seeking an experienced Policy Business Analysts to support a major government program. The role is suited to an analyst who can work across policy, legislation, and delivery environments, translating complex regulatory intent into clear, implementable business outcomes.




About the Role

As a Policy Business Analyst, you will play a critical role in bridging the gap between policy intent and delivery. You will work across policy, legal, business and technology teams to ensure legislative and regulatory requirements are clearly understood, translated, and embedded into operational and system solutions.

You will support end-to-end delivery, from interpreting legislation through to implementation, testing, and readiness, ensuring alignment with policy intent at every stage.




Key Responsibilities

Analyse legislation, regulations, and policy frameworks to understand intent, obligations, and impacts

Translate complex policy and legislative requirements into clear, traceable business requirements (functional and non-functional)

Assess impacts of policy change across people, processes, systems, data, and technology

Collaborate with stakeholders across policy, legal, delivery, and operational teams

Develop high-quality user stories, acceptance criteria, and supporting documentation

Maintain end-to-end traceability between policy intent, business requirements, and delivered solutions

Support delivery teams to ensure solutions align with legislative and policy obligations

Contribute to testing, quality assurance, and validation activities

Support change management, training, and business readiness activities




About You

You will bring a strong mix of policy interpretation and business analysis capability, within government or highly regulated environments.

Essential:

Experience analysing legislation, regulations, or policy documents and translating them into practical business requirements

Strong stakeholder engagement skills across policy, legal, delivery, and operational teams

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex concepts

Experience developing user stories, process models, impact assessments, and requirements documentation

Strong understanding of business analysis techniques (requirements elicitation, traceability, gap analysis, workflows, scenario-based testing)

Knowledge of governance, compliance, and assurance frameworks

High attention to detail, particularly in maintaining traceability and compliance

Desirable:

Experience facilitating workshops and stakeholder discussions

Experience working in Agile, hybrid, or waterfall delivery environments

Exposure to change management, training, or business readiness activities

Security & Location

This is a Canberra-based contract role, and minimum Baseline Security Clearance is required.




What We Offer

Competitive daily rates with potential contract extensions

Opportunity to work on a complex, high-impact government program

Exposure to policy-driven transformation and a nationally significant initiative

A collaborative consulting environment that values clarity, quality and practical outcomes




How to Apply

Please submit your resume and a cover letter outlining your relevant experience, work rights, and Security Clearance.

This is an opportunity to play a key role in shaping compliant, scalable solutions across a regulated environment, working closely with both business and technology stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities
Lead business analysis across multiple complex projects within a regulated PHI environment
Elicit, analyse, and document business and functional requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria using AI Prompting
Translate business needs into scalable, compliant technology solutions
Act as the bridge between stakeholders and delivery teams (business, IT, vendors, and system integrators)

Collaborate with SMEs across products, pricing, claims, operations, and compliance
Support solution design, development, testing, and implementation phases
Manage and prioritise the product/project backlog aligned to strategic and regulatory objectives
Identify process gaps, resolve data issues, and drive continuous improvement
Skills & Experience
5+ years’ experience as a Senior Business Analyst / Functional BA
Strong background in Private Health Insurance (PHI)
Products, pricing, claims, membership, and policy structures
Regulatory and compliance frameworks
Excellent documentation skills (user stories, use cases, functional specs, requirements) utlising AI Prompting
Experience across the full SDLC in Agile, Scrum, or hybrid environments
Solid understanding of system design, integrations, APIs, and databases
Proven experience working with vendors and delivery partners
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and stakeholder engagement skills
Only Sydney residents that are Citizens or Permanent Residents need apply.

The Enterprise Portfolio Management Office (EPMO) ensures Council invests in the right initiatives and realises measurable value. This role is critical in establishing disciplined benefits management, performance tracking and investment evaluation across the portfolio.
 
The Role

Maintaining portfolio cost tracking and financial performance visibility;

Coaching senior stakeholders on benefits frameworks and realisation discipline.

Ad Text:
Jacana Energy Level 4 - Remuneration Package Range $155,222 - $179,524
Finance and Analytics
Darwin
One full time ongoing vacancy is available

Jacana Energy is on an exciting journey to provide renewable energy solutions in the Territory. We’re a young business with a strong pedigree, committed to delivering outstanding service to more than 85,000 residential and commercial customers.

As a Government owned corporation, we have a solid foundation on which to build, and a compelling future to provide products and services that Territorians want and value. We are the largest energy retailer in the NT, 100% Northern Territory owned and operated, with more than 80 employees.

Why join us? 

Joining us will be an adventure. For starters you’ll be in Darwin which is unlike anywhere else in the country with plenty of unique adventures on offer. The spirit of ‘let’s give it a go’ embodied by the diverse community who call the NT home is reflected in our approach to business; we encourage new ideas; we support achievement and we’re inspired by creativity. Working with us can take you from where you are now, to where you want to be.

We’re far more than just a retailer with some ambitiously innovative projects well underway. We have a fantastic company culture and a friendly, down-to-earth executive team. You’ll never have to think about the cold or traffic ever again. 

You’ll make an impact by:

Lead and actively contribute to analytics delivery, setting team priorities, personally quality assuring code, analytics outputs, standards, and ways of working to deliver high-quality insights aligned to organisational objectives.

Implementing and/or assist in the introduction of new systems and technologies including continued management of the functions.

Uplifting capability and delivering training through strong technical proficiency and direct hands-on solution development and mentoring.

What you will bring to the team:

Demonstrated experience leading analytics, business intelligence, or data teams, including the delivery of insights using data visualisation, reporting, and analytical techniques, while remaining hands-on in the development of analytics outputs.

Understanding of the structure and layout of data systems, including databases, warehouses, and lakes with proficiency in using data analysis tools (SQL, Python) and data visualisation tools (Power BI), with demonstrated hands-on application in production environments.

Experience working with large volume, time series or operational datasets, ideally including interval, metering, or sensor based data, with direct responsibility for data transformation, validation and analysis.

Understanding of the overall architectures of cloud data platforms (emphasis on Azure) and tools and analytics platforms.

Proven ability to apply analytical thinking to complex business problems and provide clear, evidence‑based recommendations to senior stakeholders.

Sound knowledge in the rollout of organisational data governance, data quality, privacy, and ethical data use, with experience operating in regulated, public sector, or high‑accountability environments.

Highly developed communication skills with the ability to influence, negotiate, and present complex analytical concepts in plain language to diverse audiences.

Demonstrated experience leading change, improving data capability or maturity, and embedding new ways of working across an organisation.

Demonstrated experience managing competing demands, setting priorities, and delivering analytics outcomes within agreed timeframes and resources.

Next steps

If you have what it takes and are looking for a challenging role in a fast-paced environment that is focussed on growth and delivering quality service - then APPLY now

For further information about this vacancy please contact: 
William Carroll at William.Carroll@jacanaenergy.com.au

Quote vacancy number: 42260004
Closing date: 12/05/2026

Applications should consist of a one page summary sheet, resume / CV and copies of relevant qualifications.

About us

Resimac Group is a dynamic and leading ASX-listed non-bank lender and multi-channel distribution business in Australia and New Zealand.  We enable homeowners and businesses to achieve their dreams by providing innovative, fast and accessible lending solutions to homeowners, consumers and business across Australia and New Zealand. 

Our brands include Resimac, Resimac NZ, homeloans.com.au and Resimac Asset Finance and we’re proud to be servicing over 50,000+ current customers, with assets under management of over $15 billion, and a fast-growing workforce of over 330 people operating across Australia, New Zealand and the Philippines.




 

About the role

We are seeking an experienced Power BI Developer to join our Data Analytics function and play a key role in developing and maintaining the Group’s data analytics platform.

This role is focused on enabling high‑quality analytics and insightful reporting across the business, ensuring data is accessible, well‑structured and meaningful for decision‑making. You’ll work closely with finance, data and wider business stakeholders to design clean, logical data models and deliver high‑impact Power BI reports and dashboards.

It’s an ideal opportunity for an analytically minded developer who enjoys working across the business, is comfortable seeking answers from a range of stakeholders, and takes ownership of translating complex data into clear, actionable insights.

 

Key responsibilities

Reporting into the Group Head of FP&A & Data Analytics, your responsibilities will include:

Developing and maintaining the Group’s data analytics and reporting platform
Designing and building Power BI reports and dashboards using advanced DAX
Designing and developing clean, scalable data models to support reporting and visualisation
Integrating data from multiple sources to deliver enterprise‑wide insights
Developing and maintaining a UX and style guide for consistent business reporting
Managing source control and versioning for Power BI artefacts to support collaborative development
Working closely with stakeholders to gather requirements, define business logic and drive adoption of BI tools
Maintaining and enhancing existing reports to meet evolving business needs
Facilitating workshops, providing user training and supporting self‑service analytics capability
Producing clear documentation to support sustainability and knowledge sharing
Staying across emerging BI and analytics technologies and recommending improvements

 

About you

To be successful in this role, you will bring:

Strong experience developing Power BI reports and dashboards in a corporate environment
Hands‑on expertise with DAX and SQL (non‑negotiable)
Experience designing efficient, logical data models for analytics and reporting
Confidence engaging with a wide range of stakeholders and proactively seeking clarity where required
The ability to translate business requirements into clear analytical outputs
Strong attention to detail and a commitment to data quality and consistency
Experience in the financial services or finance sector will be highly regarded

 

Technical skills & experience

You’ll have strong capability across most of the following:

Power BI report and dashboard development
Advanced DAX for complex calculations and business logic
SQL for data extraction, transformation and analysis
Data modelling and semantic layer design
Source control and versioning (e.g. Git, Azure DevOps)
Snowflake or cloud data warehouse platforms (desirable)
Business communication, collaboration and requirements gathering
General technology awareness and curiosity for emerging analytics tools

 

Ways of working

Report into the Group Head of FP&A & Data Analytics
No direct reports — this is a hands‑on individual contributor role
Work closely with finance, data and technology teams across the organisation

 

Why join us

This role offers the opportunity to make a direct impact on how data is used across the organisation, contributing to:

Improved visibility and insight into business and financial performance
Stronger, more consistent reporting and analytics capability
Better‑informed decision‑making across teams

You’ll be part of a collaborative, business‑facing analytics function where your work will be used and valued.

 

What’s in it for You?

At Resimac, your growth and wellbeing matter. You’ll enjoy:
•   WFH flexibility
•   Monthly Wellness Hour – take 2 hours for you
•   Supportive, friendly and approachable team culture
•   Internal development and cross‑training opportunities
•   Reward platform with discounts, cashback and deals
•   Daily breakfast bar
•   Monthly cake day
•   Paid parental leave (16 weeks)
•   Salary packaging, EAP and external professional development

 

Resimac is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace and encourages applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, LGBTI communities, mature workers, people with disability, and people from culturally diverse backgrounds.

 

Please note that unsolicited applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted.

About Us

Fortescue is both a proud West Australian company and a global green solutions business. We are recognised for our culture, innovation and industry-leading development of infrastructure, mining assets and green energy initiatives.

Our Opportunity

Work Location: Perth Office - Fortescue's Perth office is located on the traditional lands of the Whadjuk people.

Roster: Monday to Friday

As the AI Program Lead, you will play a central role in coordinating and governing Fortescue's AI program. This role is responsible for driving program-level discipline across a portfolio of AI domains and products, ensuring the pipeline is captured, prioritised, tracked and reported effectively for senior leaders and the Board. You will bring a strong PMO and program management capability, enabling quality execution, clear governance, and transparency of progress, risks and decisions across the program.

Key to the role is supporting the Product owners on valuation and value attribution. You will work closely with product teams, Finance Business Partners, FP&A and business stakeholders to ensure value cases are robust, assumptions are sound, Fortescue value standards are consistently applied, and commitments are tracked through to realised outcomes. This role will help embed a disciplined, credible approach to measuring and reporting value from AI investments.

The AI Program Lead will work across product teams, domain leaders, finance, strategy and technology stakeholders to support the successful delivery of AI-enabled products aligned to Fortescue's business priorities.

Key Responsibilities

Manage program governance rhythms, including stage gate and investment review processes in our Steering Committee meetings, documenting actions and follow-up. Ensure program and governance standards are consistently applied across the AI portfolio
Maintain a high-quality view of the AI portfolio, including status, risks, dependencies, decisions, milestones and delivery confidence
Prepare high-quality reporting for directors, executive leaders and the Board, including updates on pipeline health, delivery progress, risks, value and key decisions required
Partner with product teams to support the development of robust business cases for AI initiatives using Fortescue value standards and frameworks, ensuring benefits are measured consistently and credibly
Work closely with Finance Business Partners, FP&A and relevant business stakeholders to validate assumptions, inputs, baselines and calculation methods
Track value commitments and delivery performance against approved expectations, highlighting variances and required interventions
Ensure value attribution is transparent, evidence-based and aligned with finance governance requirements
Identify cross-program risks, issues and interdependencies, and work with stakeholders to manage them proactively.

Qualifications and Experience

Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Engineering, Technology or a related field
Significant experience in program management, PMO, strategy, finance business partnering, or a similar role in a complex environment
Proven experience coordinating portfolios or large cross-functional programs with strong governance and executive reporting requirements
Strong capability in business case development, valuation, benefits management or value tracking
Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including experience preparing materials for senior executives and boards
Demonstrated ability to manage multiple stakeholders, drive follow-through, and improve quality and discipline across a program
Familiarity with AI, digital, technology or software delivery programs will be highly regarded
Highly organised and detail-oriented, with strong ownership and accountability for outcomes
Strong commercial and financial acumen, able to navigate ambiguity and bring clarity to complex problems
Collaborative and influential, with strong stakeholder management skills and the confidence to challenge assumptions constructively.

Our Commitment

Fortescue is deeply committed to providing a safe culture that builds respect, fosters inclusiveness, and values diversity. We celebrate individual strengths and team members from all backgrounds are encouraged to bring their whole selves to work. Our global workforce drives and promotes an inclusive culture, both within our organisation and throughout the communities we interact with across the world. Diverse backgrounds include First Nations Peoples, people with differing abilities, LGBTIQ+ community, gender, neurodiverse, cultural diversity, all age groups, and those with an intersectional or multiple diverse characteristics. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.

Position Description

This is an opportunity to join a purpose-led organisation based on the Gold Coast.

We are seeking a Finance Business Partner to support financial performance, commercial outcomes and strategic decision making across a growing portfolio.

This is a senior role within a national not-for-profit experiencing strong growth across its Queensland operations. You will partner closely with State Managers, Operations and Commercial teams to ensure financial performance, contractual compliance and long-term sustainability across your portfolio.




The Opportunity

Reporting to the Financial Controller, you will operate as a true finance partner across a diverse portfolio, supporting budgeting, performance tracking and financial decision making.

You will work across complex contractual frameworks, including funding, financing and service agreements, ensuring financial outcomes align with obligations while identifying risks, opportunities and performance improvements.

The role offers a blend of commercial insight, financial modelling and stakeholder engagement, with exposure to capital management, development activity and portfolio performance.




Key Responsibilities

Partner with State Managers and operational leaders to support financial performance
Oversee budgeting, forecasting and portfolio financial planning
Track performance against budgets and business cases, providing variance analysis and insight
Monitor financial metrics, contractual covenants and performance indicators
Review and interpret funding, financing and service agreements, ensuring financial decisions align with contractual obligations
Prepare and present financial reports and recommendations to senior stakeholders
Develop and maintain financial and operational models, including cashflow and balance sheet
Support capital management activities including drawdowns, repayments and covenant tracking
Work closely with development teams to monitor project costs and funding compliance
Ensure compliance with accounting standards, contractual obligations and internal controls
Support audits, assurance processes and continuous improvement initiatives




About You

You enjoy working across a portfolio where there is real complexity to unpack. You have a strong FP&A foundation and are confident working with stakeholders. You're comfortable interpreting detailed information contained within contracts and funding agreements, and deliver work that supports leadership with clear financial direction.




You will bring:

Minimum 3-5 yrs experience in finance business partnering, FP&A or commercial finance
Strong financial modelling and analytical capability
Ability to interpret contracts, funding agreements or complex frameworks
Confidence engaging with operational and senior stakeholders
CA or CPA qualification
High attention to detail with a proactive, solutions-focused mindset




Why This Role?

Purpose-led organisation with meaningful social impact
Growing portfolio with increasing scale and complexity
True business partnering across operations and leadership
Exposure to development projects and capital management
High visibility role supporting strategic and financial outcomes




You must hold full, unrestricted working rights in Australia. Apply now or reach out to Amy: amorrow@manselltaylor.com.au, for a confidential conversation.

Senior Corporate Reporting Analyst




We bring the energy – through purpose and people




Not just through the infrastructure we operate, but through the way we show up for each other, our customers’ and our communities. The future of energy is in our hands. Let’s shape it together.




We’re all about building an energy future to be proud of. Sound like a challenge you’ve been looking for?




Finance & Strategy plays a critical role in driving the sustainable improvement of financial performance, while ensuring strong commercial outcomes and sound risk management.

Within this, the Corporate Reporting & Analytics team delivers accurate and timely financial reporting and forecast consolidation, enabling informed decision‑making and helping drive overall financial performance.

This role is responsible for delivering high‑quality corporate reporting, including key deliverables for our controlling shareholder. You will also support forecast and budget consolidation, ensuring the integrity of financial information and providing management with meaningful insights to support effective decision‑making.

Are you looking for a purposeful role with the opportunity to grow your career and explore new challenges?

Yes? The award-winning NSW Behavioural Insights Unit are looking for you!

As part of the management team for the Behavioural Insights Unit, your role is to sharpen our focus on impact, tell our story and grow our people. There are currently five managers. Each manager brings different but complementary skills and experience to the role, ensuring our unit has leadership in design, customer experience, evaluation, data science and applied behavioural insights.

You will manage a portfolio of client-driven behavioural insights projects to provide salient advice to government. You will support project delivery with strategic judgment and operational pragmatism. As a people leader of a team of three to six people you will foster a motivated, collaborative mindset that enables innovation and personal growth.

Experience generating and using evidence to improve customer experience and placing customer needs at the heart of service improvement

The NSW Behavioural Insights Unit uses the latest behavioural science research about how people think and act to help NSW Government agencies deliver better services to citizens. Our work is driven by a commitment to evidence, ethics and empathy, with the customer and their needs at the heart of everything we do. At Rio Tinto, we value safety, teamwork, respect and inclusion. You will be part of a global organisation where your work directly impacts operations, productivity and sustainability outcomes across our Aluminium Pacific Operations.

Role is based in Brisbane


About the role


We are seeking an experienced Senior Solution Architect to find, design and implement high‑quality digital solutions to complex business problems across Rio Tinto Aluminium Pacific Operation business.


Reporting to the Manager, Digital Development, you will play a critical role in shaping end‑to‑end digital solutions by collaborating closely with business stakeholders and technical delivery teams. You will work across the full business value chain - from mining, refining through to smelting - translating business needs into scalable, secure and fit‑for‑purpose digital solutions.


This is an excellent opportunity to work in a highly collaborative environment, influencing strategic digital outcomes while remaining hands‑on in solution design and delivery.


Key accountabilities


As a Senior Solution Architect, you will:

Define, develop and confirm business and functional requirements in collaboration with business stakeholders.
Contribute to the development of the Pacific Operations Digital development strategy and roadmap.
Provide functional and solution architecture expertise to digital and broader stakeholder teams.
Deliver solution design and implementation estimates and lead software product selection, leveraging knowledge from other SME practitioners.

Partner with software engineering and delivery teams to design and implement end‑to‑end digital solutions
Manage solution overall quality assurance of the solutions, across the design, build and testing phases of delivery projects, including the review and sign off for design and build deliverables
Contribute to the design and implementation of integrated applications to drive consistency with the data models and standards.
Participate in and support any procurement related activities on system implementation vendor selection and software product selection.
Collaborate and contribute to the digital architecture forum, operational forum and other related governance forum.

What you'll bring


To be successfully considered for this role, you will have:

Bachelor's degree or higher in IT, Computer Science, Engineering or Business Information Systems or relevant disciplines.
Strong capability in translating business problems into clear, pragmatic and scalable solution designs
Proven experience collaborating with diverse stakeholders, including business leaders, engineers and external vendors
Facilitate development of project scopes, schedules, cost estimates and subsequent detailed engineering. Review contracting plans, engineering and contractor selection and procurement strategies for each initiative to ensure successful completion
Responsibilities include working with project sponsors and outside contractors to develop detailed scopes of work to include costs, preparing bid documents, establishing job execution plans, and communicating project schedules, milestones, and progress to all relevant stakeholders.
Hands on experience in solution design and/or delivery as an implementation consultant and lead solution architect across various platform, with strong ability to work with different technologies and proposed a solution.
Broad knowledge of modern digital architectures, including cloud platforms, OT/IT Systems, integration patterns, data, and application ecosystems
Experience working across full solution lifecycles, from concept and estimation through to implementation and operation
Excellent collaboration, influencing and analytical abilities.
Ability to work with multiple stakeholders in a network organisation across multiple regions/time zones.
Demonstrated ability in building good relationships with key business stakeholders.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, ability to translate complex business requirements to functional and technical solutions.


It will also be beneficial if you have:

Experience in mining, manufacturing, smelting, refining, or other asset-intensive operational environments.
Experience in project pre-sale: scoping, shaping and estimating.
Experience designing solutions across IT and OT domains.
Experience supporting vendor evaluation, procurement, and implementation partner management.
Understanding of industrial architecture and integration models, including the Purdue Model and ISA-95.
Familiarity with enterprise platforms, integration patterns, data, and analytics ecosystems.
Knowledge of cyber security, architecture governance, and risk considerations in industrial environments.
Experience working in Agile or product-based delivery environments.
Experience in support modeling and service industrialisation.
Experience working with enterprise architecture frameworks such as TOGAF, or other structured architecture methodologies.
Exposure to cloud platforms such as Azure and/or AWS, including designing secure and scalable cloud-based solutions.
Relevant architecture, cloud, or delivery certifications.

#gts

About Rio Tinto

Rio Tinto is a leading global mining and materials company. We operate in 35 countries where we produce iron ore, copper, aluminium, critical minerals, and other materials needed for the global energy transition and for people, communities, and nations to thrive.

We have been mining for 150 years and operate with knowledge built up across generations and continents. Our purpose is finding better ways to provide the materials the world needs - striving for innovation and continuous improvement to produce materials with low emissions and to the right environmental, social and governance standards. But we can't do it on our own, so we're focused on creating partnerships to solve problems, create win-win situations and meet opportunities.

Respect and Inclusion

At Rio Tinto, we particularly welcome and encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, women, the LGBTI+ community, mature workers, people with disabilities and people from different cultural backgrounds.

We are committed to an inclusive environment where people feel comfortable to be themselves. We want our people to feel that all voices are heard, all cultures respected and that a variety of perspectives are not only welcome - they are essential to our success. We treat each other fairly and with dignity regardless of race, gender, nationality, ethnic origin, religion, age, sexual orientation or anything else that makes us different.

Shape the Future of Energy Pricing in Tasmania.


Join TasNetworks as a Specialist Pricing Analyst and play a key role in designing smarter, fairer, and future-ready network tariffs. This is a unique opportunity to blend advanced analytics, economics, and real-world impact in a rapidly evolving energy landscape.

As a Specialist Pricing Analyst, you'll turn complex data into actionable insights that influence how electricity networks are priced and managed. Working within a dynamic regulatory environment, you'll help design innovative tariff structures that support a safe, reliable network while preparing for the energy transition.

You'll collaborate across teams and with external stakeholders, applying analytical rigour and commercial insight to solve challenging problems and shape strategic outcomes.

You will be responsible for, but not limited to -

Combining data analytics and economic/commercial expertise to deliver insights that inform innovative network tariff design
Providing expert advice on pricing and network planning in a complex, evolving regulatory environment
Monitoring tariff developments across the National Electricity Market to inform strategy
Translating complex data and regulatory frameworks into clear, persuasive insights for stakeholders
Supporting the Regulated Pricing Team in developing solutions aligned with tariff strategy and regulatory requirements

About you

You bring strong analytical capability and a proven ability to turn complex data into meaningful insights that inform business decisions. You're comfortable working at the intersection of data, economics, and strategy, and can clearly communicate technical outcomes to a wide range of stakeholders.

You will possess -

Significant experience in quantitative modelling and advanced data analysis
Proven ability to analyse large datasets, develop models, and apply analytics techniques to solve complex business problems
Strong capability to synthesise complex information, generate insights, and communicate clear recommendations
Solid numerical and statistical skills, including an understanding of machine learning concepts and when to apply them
Experience using tools such as Python, R, SQL, Power BI (or comparable) for data analysis and visualisation
Ability to identify and validate new data sources, and improve existing data for analysis and reporting
Strong time management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across technical and non-technical teams and engage external stakeholders

You'll also hold a relevant tertiary qualification in a numerical or analytical discipline such as economics, commerce, engineering, or science or bring 5+ years of equivalent practical experience.

What's on offer

This is a permanent full-time opportunity based out of Hobart, offering an attractive remuneration and benefits package.

At TasNetworks we want to power a bright future for our people, our community and our customers. We offer a supportive work environment where safety is at the heart of everything we do.

As an "employer of choice" we offer excellent employee benefits including -

Free onsite car parking
20 days of annual leave per year + an additional 3 days of leave between Christmas and New Year
Electricity discount of 16.66%
17.5% leave loading (paid annually)
Career development - we have our own RTO and can create personalised career pathways in both trade and leadership
Onsite gyms (plus an annual health and wellbeing rebate)
All the tools and equipment you'll need to be successful

We are proud to be work180 accredited, check us out here.

How to apply

Please ensure you attach a cover letter outlining how your skills and experience are aligned with this role, and a current resume. For more information about this opportunity and to acquire a position description, please click here.

Applications close 11:59pm Sunday 17 May 2026, however, we will be reviewing applications upon receipt and may commence our recruitment process prior to the closing date.

TasNetworks is an Equal Opportunity employer and is committed to responding to every applicant. We encourage applications from all members of the community, including people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds and mature aged people.

About Us

TasNetworks is Powering a Bright Future by delivering safe and sustainable essential services while maximising value for all of Lutruwita/Tasmania.

As Tasmania's provider of electricity transmission and distribution services, we are committed to providing our customers with affordable and reliable electricity, while helping Australia transition to cleaner energy sources. We have 300,000 residential, business, commercial, and industrial customers, and are committed to working with our community to make a meaningful difference to the lives of Tasmanians - through and beyond the delivery of electricity and telecommunication services.

By joining TasNetworks, you will experience the difference of a company that puts customers first, values safety above all, unifies in the face of challenges, and embraces diversity and forward thinking.

Together, we are powering a bright future for Lutruwita/Tasmania.

Job Title: SAP BTP CPI Integration Specialist - Enterprise Transformation
Ad Text:
Join a high-performing integration practice delivering large-scale digital transformation across Australia's most complex enterprise environments. This is a hands-on role where you'll shape modern integration landscapes using SAP's latest cloud technologies.
 
The Opportunity
 
You will design and deliver robust, scalable integrations across SAP and non-SAP systems, playing a critical role in modernising enterprise platforms and enabling real-time, API-driven architectures.
 
What You'll Be Doing

Designing and building integration flows using SAP BTP Integration Suite (CPI)
Developing and enhancing iFlows, mappings (Groovy, XSLT, Java), and adapters
Integrating SAP with external platforms via REST, SOAP, IDoc, RFC, and OData
Supporting PI/PO to CPI migration programs
Managing monitoring, performance optimisation, and error handling
Working closely with architects and business stakeholders to deliver fit-for-purpose integration solutions
What You'll Bring

5+ years SAP integration experience across PI/PO and CPI
Strong hands-on development experience within SAP BTP Integration Suite
Proven capability integrating SAP with complex enterprise ecosystems
Experience with API Management and event-driven architectures
Solid understanding of security (OAuth, certificates, authentication)
Strong communication skills and ability to operate in delivery-focused teams
Top Reasons to Join

Attractive compensation & rewards aligned to the Australian market with performance incentives and recognition programs
Global exposure with local impact, working on major Australian programs backed by international expertise
Empowering leadership & supportive environment where innovation and initiative are genuinely valued
Long-term career growth through continuous learning, certifications, and clear progression pathways
Inclusive, collaborative culture that values diverse thinking and high performance
Why This Role:


This is not a BAU integration role. You'll be at the forefront of cloud-first transformation, working on complex, high-value programs that demand modern thinking and technical excellence.

Leverage data to optimise supply chain commercial business 

12 month fixed-term contract (full-time hours)

Norwest location




Welcome to Primary Connect

Primary Connect is the most extensive food and retail supply chain network in Australia and New Zealand. Our 10,000-strong team are an essential part of the expansive Woolworths Group, which encompasses Woolworths Supermarkets and Metro stores, BIG W, Countdown NZ, Quantium, and more. With us, your possibilities are not limited to a single opportunity. 




We’re delivering to more than 3,000 retail stores every day (stores like Woolworths, BIG W, Dan Murphy’s, and BWS), but at Primary Connect, we’re more than just a supply chain business. We’re a big team, but every individual plays a pivotal role and leaves a distinctive impact. 




Our extensive reach opens up boundless opportunities for learning, growth, and professional development. By raising your hand, fostering relationships, and seizing the moment, you can take control of your own unique path. 




What you’ll do

Working within Primary Connect's International Supply Chain team, you will be the analytical engine driving commercial and operational decision-making. You will decode complex logistics data, uncover commercial opportunities, and streamline our international supply chain operations.

Onboarding new PC+ customers to ISC CW systems and establish the charge codes and accurate rate cards for “cost” and “sell rate”

Manage the costing and revenue registration of the International Supply Chain activities for PC+ customers. Validate the accuracy of the cost and sell rates

Commercial & pricing analysis to identify cost-saving and optimisation opportunities. Developing, testing and implementing new cost / revenue / invoice solutions for PC+ customers.

Actionable insights and reporting of data into clear outputs and reports that guide strategic decision-making for senior stakeholders.

Continuous Improvement: Proactively identify bottlenecks and partner with cross-functional teams to design and implement process improvements.

Act as a key liaison between supply chain operations, finance, and external logistics partners to ensure alignment on performance metrics and commercial targets.




What you’ll bring

You are commercially astute, highly analytical, and thrive in a fast-paced logistics environment. You are curious and solutions-focused, with a a passion for turning complex data into a compelling narrative.

Domain Expertise: Experienced business analyst , ideally with a strong background in supply chain, logistics, freighting, or retail.

High-level proficiency in data analysis, financial modelling, and data visualisation tools (e.g., Excel, SQL, Tableau, or PowerBI).

Proven track record of identifying commercial opportunities, driving pricing strategies, and delivering tangible cost efficiencies.

Strong stakeholder management skills with the ability to confidently present complex data insights to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Strong background in AP/AR and Tertiary Degree with finance related background (preferrably CPA)




What you’ll experience

Everything we do is driven by a culture of care for our team, customers, and community. 

We create a thriving team experience through a culture of belonging that champions safety and wellbeing for our people and partners.

We’re a team-first organisation. We’ll equip you with the necessary training for success in your role, and provide continuous career development and learning opportunities. 

We offer competitive remuneration and access to a number of employee benefits.

We prioritise sustainability, striving to contribute to a greener, better future. Check out our most recent Sustainability Report to find out how we do it.

A range of programs to help you prioritise and manage your wellbeing, including a competitive leave policy and 24/7 access to the Sonder app.




Want to know what it’s like to have a career with us? Check out this short summary of the career of one of our excellent 2022 Team Member of the Year, Jesna.




Everyone belongs at Woolworths Group

Diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging are key to realising our purpose of better together for a better tomorrow. We recognise the value our team’s diversity brings to our business, customers, and communities and that teams with diverse experiences and backgrounds enrich our group and are better able to innovate and solve problems. As one of the largest employers in Australia and New Zealand, we aim to create a truly inclusive workplace where everyone feels that they belong, can be their best selves, and reach their full potential.




We encourage all candidates to apply; please let us know in your application if we can support you with any adjustments in the hiring process.




See how we’re creating better experiences together, for a better tomorrow

Discover more opportunities with Woolworths Group

Ad Text:
Quality, Process & Integration Lead

Executive Level 2

Melbourne or Brisbane, other Bureau capital city locations will be considered

Position overview

The Quality, Process and Integration Lead sits within the Bureau's Services Development and Integration (SDI) section – part of the Community Services Group (CSG) and is the Team Lead of the Operations Support Team (OST).

The SDI Teams provide operations support services for the whole of CSG. These support services include rostering, resource coordination, quality management, process management, integration with other areas of the Bureau, and project delivery.

The Team Lead will lead a multidisciplinary team of specialists providing those quality management, performance management, lessons management and integration support functions across the critical service delivery areas in CSG. This enables weather and warning services to be provided in the most efficient and effective manner to the Australian Community and other customers, as well as being continuously benchmarked and improved over time.

The role will report to the Manager, Services Development and Integration, and will work with them to oversee and coordinate:

Implementation, support, maintenance and improvement of Quality Management initiatives in CSG (including but not limited to performance verification/reporting and lessons management/debriefing).
Contribute to process improvement initiatives in CSG.
Product consolidation and business simplification initiatives.
Operational integration between CSG and other Groups in the Bureau.
Business alignment and change aspects associated with new or updated software applications in CSG.
Maintain consistent communication channels across operational teams and stakeholders. Support coordination of systems, data, and ICT changes to ensure continuity and visibility of operational impacts

The successful candidate will:

exemplify customer focus as the heart of everything that we do.
practice inclusive leadership and relentless enterprise collaboration in support of delivering outcomes against the Bureau's strategic pillars of Impact and Value, Operational Excellence, Innovation and Insight, the Bureau Way.
demonstrate sound judgement and capitalise on innovative alternatives to resolve complex problems.
challenge the status quo when required and demonstrate resilience and personal accountability for the quality of advice and delivery of results.

To be a strong contender for the role you will need to have a strong understanding of operational service delivery environments, including experience in an organisation delivering scientific or environmental services. You will have a track record of achievement in embedding transformation outcomes and continuous improvement initiatives in scientific or highly technically based organisations.

As an outstanding people leader in complex and dynamic operating environments, you will have a demonstrated capacity to work with geographically distributed multidisciplinary teams within an operational customer centric environment. You will be able to point to significant success in leadership, including developing high performing teams and working successfully with peers. These qualities will also allow you to work seamlessly with the Bureau’s customers, partners and stakeholders, with the objective of understanding and articulating complex business needs and delivering relevant and high-value products and services. You will also clearly demonstrate that you can positively contribute to the overall leadership of the Community Services Group, supporting the Manager, Services Development and Integration, and the CSG Leadership Team.

Application Closing Date: Wednesday, 13 May 2026

For further information please review the position description on https://bomcareers.nga.net.au/?jati=46FD9D76-4AA0-3B4E-A6E0-ECDAB6CA52DE

About Us

Dyson is a global technology enterprise. We solve the problems others choose to ignore, with surprising new inventions that defy convention and simply work better. We’re growing fast and our ambition is huge – more categories, more locations and more people.

About the Role

As our Ecommerce Intelligence Lead, you will be the architect of our digital engine room. Your mission is to pinpoint the commercial moments that matter, from conversion and traffic to pricing and customer journeys and harness dormant data to drive exponential growth.

This isn’t a role for incremental tweaks; it’s about challenging assumptions and using AI and automation to rewire our ways of working for pace and efficiency. By turning analytics into a force multiplier, you will empower our team to work smarter and faster, ensuring we don’t just stay ahead of the AI curve, but define it.

Main responsibilities include but not limited to:-

Partner with Digital Leaders across Trading, CRM, and Performance to identify growth drivers and unlock untapped data opportunities

Map the full end-to-end customer journey, stitching together disconnected data sets to identify and fix friction points and leaky funnels

Pinpoint where AI and automation can drive commercial growth, operational efficiency, or a superior customer experience

Rapidly prototype, test, and implement tools and scripts—such as advanced price-scrapers and sentiment analysis bots—to solve real-world business problems

Build and launch a Command Centre dashboard with real-time trading data and automated alerts to prompt immediate team action

Bridge the gap between technical and commercial teams by demystifying AI and inspiring a test and learn culture

Audit and automate manual workflows, specifically targeting DTC stock management and paid search tasks to significantly reduce manual effort

Deploy optimisation experiments, including personalised landing content and automated offer testing, and share actionable insights

Surface CRM and loyalty insights to highlight potential for customer retention and lifecycle improvements

Embed new tools into day-to-day team processes and drive active adoption through workshops and mentoring

Track and communicate the tangible value delivered, focusing on revenue lift, time saved, and KPI improvements

Create a Global Playbook to summarise successful solutions and provide a roadmap for scaling across other markets and categories

Deliver comprehensive documentation to ensure all created tools are sustainably managed and owned by the business

About You

You are a commercially minded professional who stays relentlessly focused on the drivers of tangible results. A collaborative partner by nature, you work seamlessly across technical, commercial, and marketing teams to bridge gaps and drive collective success. You prioritise practical application over innovation theatre, ensuring every solution is built for real-world usage and high adoption. You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and maintain a strong bias for action, thriving in an autonomous environment where you can proactively hunt for opportunities and deliver solutions without requiring constant guidance.

Previous skills & experience:-

Experience with Ecommerce trading levers - conversion, traffic, pricing, merchandising, CRM, and customer experience

Track record of driving measurable outcomes through automation, analytics, or AI - ideally where speed and experimentation matter

Hands-on with automation and analytics tech. But you measure success in outcomes, not algorithms

Benefits

Outside of a competitive salary, our team members receive generous product discounts, additional paid annual leave, a generous, above market parental leave scheme and ongoing learning and development opportunities.

At Dyson, our people are at the heart of everything we do. We value you bringing yourself to work and we want to deliver an employee experience that matches the quality we give to our customers.

We have a vibrant and diverse culture that is geared towards recognition and realising ambition. You'll have regular social activities to take part in and you'll work with a fantastic team of people every day.

About Us

At City Beach, our people are at the core of what we do, both our customers and our team. All are welcome under the City Beach roof, where value is placed on individual style, teamwork, authenticity, adaptability, and a desire to exceed expectations. 

We stock some of the world's biggest brands across surf, skate, streetwear, and fashion. Our stores are a hub that connect our customers to events, influencers, and trends, allowing them to live life on their terms. 


Position

The Technical Business Analyst plays a key role in shaping our retail technology by delivering and enhancing our core business systems, including Point of Sale (POS), Product Information Management (PIM), Master Data Management (MDM), and the Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central ERP. The role is responsible for discovering opportunities for change, streamlining processes, and supporting seamless system integrations that improve the City Beach experience from retail stores through to head office.


Key Responsibilities

Partner with stakeholders across Retail, Supply Chain, Finance, and eCommerce to understand business needs and translate them into technical requirements.

Analyse and document current processes, workflows, and data flows to support effective solution design and delivery.

Collaborate with wider teams to ensure system configurations, integrations, and enhancements align with business goals.

Support testing activities including UAT planning, execution, and resolution of issues to support project delivery.

Assist with deployment readiness, cutover planning, and post go-live support for system initiatives.

Work closely with developers and architects to maintain data accuracy, consistency, and integration across platforms.

Support data migration and validation activities to ensure integrity across connected systems.

Maintain clear and accurate documentation including user stories, process maps, and technical specifications.

Contribute to change management and governance processes to ensure quality, traceability, and alignment with business priorities.

Identify risks and improvement opportunities, helping to drive efficiency and continuous improvement.




Skills & Experience

3–5 years’ experience as a Business Analyst, ideally within retail or a fast-paced consumer environment.

Solid understanding of retail systems and processes including POS, product lifecycle, pricing, inventory, and fulfilment.

Experience delivering tech solutions such as POS, PIM/MDM, or ERP platforms (preferably Microsoft Dynamics 365 Business Central).

Strong knowledge of integrations including APIs, EDI, and master data flows.

Confident in gathering and documenting requirements through user stories and process mapping.

Experience in testing, UAT support, and cross-functional delivery.

Clear communicator who builds strong relationships with both technical and business teams.

Familiarity with tools such as Confluence, JIRA, Figma, or Visio is a plus.




Why Join Us

40% team member discount for you, your friends and family to access.

Access to our Beach Benefits Program where we have partnered with industry leaders to bring our team members discounts on products and memberships to keep you healthy on all fronts.

Access to Psych Health our Employee Assistance Program.

Prezzee Gift Cards for major milestones and yearly anniversary celebrations.

Weekly shared morning tea to celebrate team member milestones.

Friday 4pm finishes and social drinks every week.

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:


Thrive in an innovative, collaborative people culture
Be part of market-leading projects with global scale and complexity
Enjoy 18 weeks paid parental leave plus 12 months of return-to-work support (work 4, get paid 5)

Accelerate your career with hands-on projects across diverse teams, industry-leading actuarial and project management training, and standout study support to help you grow into a well-rounded, innovative problem solver.

We offer great study support packages for relevant qualifications along with a range of technical and non-technical training opportunities.

Deloitte Investments and Superannuation Advisory team is recognised for providing clients with a comprehensive range of high value advisory and consulting services. Working with some of Australia's major financial services institutions, superannuation funds and service providers, we have achieved significant growth over recent years. This growth has created a number of opportunities within our team.

Enough about us, let's talk about you

Why Deloitte?

We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - whether it be up, moving into something new, or even moving across the world.   

We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, with different experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.  

We value in-person connection with our clients and our colleagues. We offer several ways for you to work flexibly so that you can serve your clients, stay connected with your team, and manage your personal priorities.

We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.  

 Next Steps

Company Description


Supporting millions of Aussies since 1988 with low fees and competitive long-term performance. Profits back to members, not shareholders

Closing date: Wednesday 13th May 2026.

Please note Rest does not accept speculative resumes from recruitment agencies 

Rest will review applications prior to the closing date and may close the role earlier 

Job Description


Established in 1988, Rest is one of Australia’s largest profit-to-member superannuation funds.

We support more than two million members, with around $100 billion of funds under management and are recognised as a responsible investment leader*. We believe when members understand and engage with their super, they’re more likely to get a better retirement outcome.

Everything we do at Rest is underpinned by our values and behaviours, we want to Be Daring, Keep it Simple, Take Action and Have Grit. To put it simply we want our people to thrive and love the work they do.

The Senior Manager, Member Strategy is responsible for shaping Rest’s member and employer acquisition and retention strategies in alignment with the group strategy. It is a key connecting role between the group strategy and Member teams, hence with a direct reporting line to the GM, Member Value and a dotted line to the GM Strategy. The role brings together external market insights, Voice of Customer (VOC) analysis and portfolio analytics to ensure the delivery of Rest’s strategy is meaningfully shaped by the member voice and focused on the strongest value creation for members. The role also provides strategic guidance to the Member division leadership team on specific focus areas and initiatives.

Key Accountabilities/Responsibilities

Develop the member and employer acquisition, growth and retention plans ensuring alignment with Rest’s purpose and group strategy, and Member divisional operational requirements.
Monitor and analyse external market trends, competitor activity, regulatory developments and demographic shifts to proactively shape member and employer strategy and planning.
Translate enterprise strategy and market insights into clear, actionable strategic plans that support long-term member value.
Design and refine strategies to drive member acquisition, employer channel growth and improved member engagement across Rest’s diverse membership segments.
Design and deliver customer segmentation strategy, driving alignment to segment priorities and opportunities across the enterprise.
Establish data‑led retention strategies, identifying key drivers of member attrition and developing interventions to improve loyalty and tenure.
Partner with Strategy, Finance and relevant Member teams to develop business cases to support strategic recommendations.
Partner with Experience Design and Journeys teams to ensure member strategies translate into coherent, high-impact experiences across all channels.
Provide strategic advice to the GM Member Value, CMO, GM Strategy and leadership teams on member and employer value, growth opportunities and risk considerations.
Support enterprise prioritisation by advocating for the member voice across forums, governance bodies, and planning processes.
Contribute to preparation of strategy papers, Board/Committee materials and executive updates to ensure consistency, quality, and alignment.
Contribute to an engaged, high-performing team environment that promotes collaboration, accountability, and continuous learning.

Qualifications


Relevant Business, Commerce or Finance degree.

Required experience, understanding or credentials including:

Financial Services Expertise: demonstrable experience within superannuation or financial services, with a deep understanding of member, product and regulatory environments.
Core Strategic Capability and Skills: Demonstrable consulting experience with a focus in problem solving, strategy development and ability to support strategic thinking, ideally with experience in Customer Strategy, Go to Market Strategy and Growth.
Strategic and Commercial Acumen: Proven ability to apply commercial judgment to shape strategic direction, make critical decisions and deliver outcomes to defined timeframes.
Financial Acumen: Proven ability to model and demonstrate financial outcomes of strategic recommendations.
Innovation and Problem Solving: With applied experience in HCD and customer-led strategy to bring creativity and complex problem-solving skills, and the ability to design and deliver innovative solutions that enhance member experience.
AI and Digital Mindset: Passion for leveraging data, analytics, and emerging technologies to uncover insights, measure performance, and inform continuous improvement.
Collaborative Leadership: Strong influencing skills with the ability to lead through others, fostering alignment and collaboration across diverse functions and teams. Demonstrated ability to manage senior stakeholders.
Attention to Detail and Perspective: Balances strategic vision with operational precision, ensuring initiatives are both high-impact and well-executed.
Effective Communication: Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex insights and strategies into clear, compelling narratives.
Resilience and Prioritisation: Skilled in managing multiple priorities in complex, fast-paced environments while maintaining focus on strategic outcomes and member value.

Additional Information


Our benefits have been designed so you can tailor your experience with us and include:

Personal and professional development opportunities 
Hybrid working 
Purchase leave scheme and gender neutral 16 weeks paid parental leave 
Super Contribution Continuation for 12 Months of parental leave 
Linkedin Learning 
Income Protection Insurance 
Rest Excellence awards (peer recognition awards based on Rest’s values and behaviours) 
Rest Stops - meeting free breaks 

If you share our values, believe you can help make a difference for our members and want to be part of a leading superannuation fund with a Super culture, please click Apply Now.

Rest is committed to creating a flexible work environment and culture that embraces diversity, equity, and inclusion - where people feel welcome, safe to be themselves and inspired to do their best.
